entries.definition:
th–b–ṭ to be slow, sluggish, phlegmatic; to cause someone to
lose heart, to discourage, to turn away. Of this root, ثَبَّطَ thabbaṭa
occurs once in the Qur’an.

ثَبَّطَ thabbaṭa [v. II, trans.] to frustrate, to discourage, to
impede; to cause someone to be reticent (9:46) وَلَكِنْ كَرِهَ اللَّهُ انْبِعَاثَهُمْ
فَثَبَّطَهُمْ وَقِيلَ اقْعُدُوا مَعَ الْقَاعِدِينَ but God was averse to their going forth,
so He discouraged them, and it was said, ‘Stay behind with the
laggards.’
